programs.tagline: Empowering rural women to become business leaders

articles.title: How Rural Women Are Redefining Entrepreneurship in Rajasthan
articles.excerpt: A look at three women-led enterprises that emerged from our SHG network, transforming local economies one small business at a time.
articles.content:
Self-Help Groups (SHGs) have emerged as one of the most effective vehicles for women's empowerment in rural India. This study examines the economic and social impact of SHGs on rural women in Rajasthan over a five-year period (2020-2025).

Key Findings

Our research tracked 500 women across 40 SHGs in Bhilwara, Udaipur, and Chittorgarh districts. The findings reveal significant improvements across multiple dimensions of empowerment:

  • Income Growth: Average household income increased by 45% over the study period.
  • Financial Inclusion: 92% of members now have bank accounts, compared to 34% before joining.
  • Decision-Making: 78% of women reported greater participation in household financial decisions.
"Before joining the SHG, I never had a say in how our family money was spent. Now I not only manage our household finances but also run a small tailoring business."
— Lakshmi Devi, SHG Member, Bhilwara

articles.title: The State of Microfinance in India: 2026 Outlook
articles.excerpt: An analysis of current trends, challenges, and opportunities in India's microfinance sector with implications for rural development.
articles.content:
In this research study, Dr. Ananya Rao explores the critical dimensions of microfinance in India through the lens of ongoing work in Rajasthan. The data collected from rural communities reveal important patterns and actionable insights.

Key Findings

Our analysis draws from field observations, community feedback, and systematic assessment of program outcomes.

  • Community Ownership: Programs with strong community participation show 3x better outcomes.
  • Women's Leadership: Women-led initiatives demonstrate remarkable effectiveness in driving community development.

articles.title: Explainer: What Is a Self-Help Group?
articles.excerpt: A beginner-friendly guide to understanding SHGs — their structure, benefits, and role in rural financial inclusion.
articles.content:
A Self-Help Group (SHG) is a village-based financial intermediary committee usually composed of 10-25 local women. Members make small regular savings contributions over a few months until there is enough capital in the group to begin lending.

How SHGs Work

Funds may then be lent back to the members or to others in the village for any purpose. In India, many SHGs are 'linked' to banks for the delivery of microcredit.

articles.title: Digital Literacy Transforming Rural Entrepreneurship
articles.excerpt: Exploring the role of digital skills in enabling rural entrepreneurs to access markets, manage finances, and grow their businesses.
articles.content: Digital literacy is transforming how rural entrepreneurs operate their businesses. From using UPI for payments to leveraging social media for marketing, women entrepreneurs are embracing technology. Our programs have trained over 10,000 women in digital skills, leading to a 60% adoption rate of digital payments among participants.

articles.title: Policy Recommendations for Rural Financial Inclusion
articles.excerpt: Evidence-based policy recommendations for improving access to financial services in underserved rural areas.
articles.content: Based on our research across 12 districts in Rajasthan, we present key policy recommendations for strengthening rural financial inclusion.

reports.title: Impact of SHG-Bank Linkage on Rural Household Income
reports.author: Dr. Ananya Rao
reports.summary: An empirical study across 12 districts showing a 22% average income increase post SHG-bank linkage.
